annotations.label: Fundamental group (informal)

anchor.snippet: the fundamental group of a topological space is the group of the equivalence classes under homotopy of the loops contained in the space

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroup X x` is defined as `End (FundamentalGroupoid.mk x)`, matching the informal definition.

annotations.label: Homotopy invariance of fundamental group

anchor.snippet: topological spaces that are homotopy equivalent (or the stronger case of homeomorphic ) have isomorphic fundamental groups

annotations.note: Mathlib has the equivalence of fundamental groupoids under homotopy equivalence, but no direct `MulEquiv` of fundamental groups extracted from it.

annotations.label: Loop based at a point

anchor.snippet: a loop based at

annotations.note: A loop at `x` is `Path x x`, abbreviated `LoopSpace X x` (notation `Ω`).

annotations.label: Homotopy of loops

anchor.snippet: A homotopy is a continuous interpolation between two loops

annotations.note: `Path.Homotopy p₀ p₁` is the type of homotopies between two paths (loops) relative to endpoints.

anchors.snippet: A homotopy is a continuous interpolation between two loops

annotations.label: Homotopy is an equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: is an equivalence relation so that the set of equivalence classes can be considered

annotations.note: Homotopy relation on paths is proved an equivalence with `Path.Homotopic.equivalence` and the corresponding `Setoid`.

anchors.snippet: is an equivalence relation so that the set of equivalence classes can be considered

annotations.label: Fundamental group at a base point

anchor.snippet: This set (with the group structure described below) is called the fundamental group of the topological space

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroup X x` carries a `Group` instance via the End-monoid of the fundamental groupoid.

anchors.snippet: This set (with the group structure described below) is called the fundamental group of the topological space

annotations.label: Product of loops (concatenation)

anchor.snippet: their product is defined as the loop

annotations.note: Concatenation of paths is `Path.trans`, used to build the group operation on the fundamental groupoid.

annotations.label: Well-defined product on homotopy classes

anchor.snippet: It can be shown that this product does not depend on the choice of representatives and therefore gives a well-defined operation

annotations.note: Composition on `Path.Homotopic.Quotient` is defined via `Quotient.map₂` using `Path.Homotopic.hcomp` (the product is well-defined).

annotations.label: Group structure on fundamental group

anchor.snippet: This operation turns

annotations.note: A `Group` instance on `FundamentalGroup X x` is provided via `End` of the fundamental groupoid.

annotations.label: Associativity up to homotopy

anchor.snippet: The associativity axiom

annotations.note: `Path.Homotopic.trans_assoc` provides the homotopy realizing associativity of path concatenation.

annotations.label: Base point independence for path-connected spaces

anchor.snippet: this choice makes no difference as long as the space

annotations.note: `fundamentalGroupMulEquivOfPathConnected` gives a `MulEquiv` between fundamental groups at any two basepoints in a path-connected space.

annotations.label: Trivial fundamental group of Euclidean / contractible spaces

anchor.snippet: in Euclidean space

annotations.note: Contractible spaces are simply connected via `SimplyConnectedSpace.ofContractible`, and `RealTopologicalVectorSpace.contractibleSpace` makes Euclidean spaces contractible.

annotations.label: Simply connected space

anchor.snippet: A path-connected space whose fundamental group is trivial is called simply connected

annotations.note: `SimplyConnectedSpace X` is defined via the fundamental groupoid being equivalent to `Discrete Unit`.

annotations.label: Spheres are simply connected

anchor.snippet: the 2-sphere

annotations.note: Only a `proof_wanted` exists for the 3-sphere in `PoincareConjecture`; the simple connectedness of `Sⁿ` for n ≥ 2 is not formalized.

annotations.note: No `FundamentalGroup` computation for `Circle`/`S¹` is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of the figure eight

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group of the figure eight is the free group on two letters

annotations.note: Figure-eight space and its fundamental group are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.note: No formalization of the bouquet of circles or its free fundamental group.

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group of a wedge sum of two path connected spaces

anchor.snippet: the fundamental group of a graph is a free group

annotations.note: There is no fundamental-group treatment of topological graphs in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: suppose

annotations.label: Knot group

anchor.snippet: Knot groups are by definition the fundamental group of the complement of a knot

annotations.note: Knots and knot groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Knot group of the trefoil

anchor.snippet: the knot group of the trefoil knot is known to be the braid group

annotations.note: Neither the trefoil knot nor its knot group is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of an orientable surface

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group of a genus- n orientable surface can be computed in terms of generators and relations

annotations.note: Genus-`n` surfaces and their surface-group presentation are not formalized.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of the torus

annotations.label: Fundamental group of a topological group is abelian

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group of a topological group

annotations.note: Although `HSpace` is defined and `IsTopologicalGroup.hSpace` is an instance, no theorem deduces commutativity of `FundamentalGroup` from this.

annotations.label: Eckmann–Hilton argument for H-spaces

anchor.snippet: the Eckmann–Hilton argument shows that it does in fact agree with the above concatenation of loops

annotations.note: The abstract Eckmann–Hilton argument is formalized and used in `HomotopyGroup` to prove `π_(n+2)` is commutative, but not specifically applied to H-spaces' fundamental groups.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of an H-space is abelian

anchor.snippet: is abelian for any H-space

annotations.note: `HSpace` is defined but no theorem states the fundamental group of an H-space is commutative.

annotations.label: Induced homomorphism

anchor.snippet: The resulting group homomorphism , called the induced homomorphism

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroup.map f x` is the `MonoidHom` induced by a continuous map `f` on fundamental groups.

annotations.label: Fundamental group is a functor

anchor.snippet: the formation of associating to a topological space its fundamental group is therefore a functor

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the fundamental groupoid as a functor `TopCat ⥤ Grpd`; `FundamentalGroup.map` provides the pointed/group-level version.

annotations.label: Homotopy equivalent spaces have isomorphic fundamental groups

anchor.snippet: two homotopy equivalent path-connected spaces have isomorphic fundamental groups

annotations.note: Equivalence of fundamental groupoids under homotopy equivalence is formalized; the resulting `MulEquiv` on `FundamentalGroup` is not extracted as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Fundamental group preserves products and coproducts

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group functor takes products to products and coproducts to coproducts

annotations.note: Product preservation (`piIso`, `prodIso`, `preservesProduct`) is in Mathlib; the coproduct/wedge case is not.

annotations.label: Seifert–van Kampen theorem

anchor.snippet: a special case of the Seifert–van Kampen theorem , which states that the fundamental group functor takes pushouts along inclusions to pushouts

annotations.note: Seifert–van Kampen is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hurewicz theorem (first homology)

anchor.snippet: A special case of the Hurewicz theorem asserts that the first singular homology group

annotations.note: No formalization of the Hurewicz theorem (n=1 or general) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First homology is the abelianization

anchor.snippet: is isomorphic to the abelianization of the fundamental group

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Abelianization` but no link `H₁(X) ≅ Abelianization (π₁ X)`.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of a wedge as free product

anchor.snippet: is the free product of the fundamental groups of the

annotations.note: No statement that the fundamental group of a wedge is a free product is in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: Any path connected, locally path connected and locally simply connected topological space

annotations.label: Long exact sequence of a fibration

anchor.snippet: The great importance of fibrations to the computation of homotopy groups stems from a long exact sequence

annotations.label: Every finitely presented group is a fundamental group of a finite simplicial complex

anchor.section: Edge-path group of a simplicial complex
anchor.snippet: every finitely presented group arises as the fundamental group of a finite simplicial complex

annotations.note: No realization theorem for finitely presented groups by simplicial complexes in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every group is the fundamental group of a 2-dimensional CW-complex

anchor.snippet: Every group can be realized as the fundamental group of a connected CW-complex of dimension 2 (or higher)

annotations.note: CW-complex realizability of arbitrary groups is not formalized; Mathlib lacks CW-complex API for this.

annotations.label: Finitely presented groups realized by 4-manifolds

anchor.snippet: Every finitely presented group can be realized as the fundamental group of a compact

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Restrictions on 3-manifold fundamental groups

anchor.snippet: no free abelian group of rank 4 or higher can be realized as the fundamental group of a manifold of dimension 3 or less

annotations.note: No 3-manifold fundamental-group restrictions in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Realization in compact Hausdorff spaces and measurable cardinals

anchor.snippet: every group can be realized as the fundamental group of a compact Hausdorff space if and only if there is no measurable cardinal

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: the Hurewicz theorem implies that for all

annotations.label: Loop space

anchor.snippet: is known as the loop space

annotations.note: `LoopSpace X x := Path x x` is the based loop space, with notation `Ω`.

annotations.label: Fundamental groupoid

anchor.snippet: The fundamental groupoid is a variant of the fundamental group that is useful in situations where the choice of a base point

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroupoid X` is defined with a `Groupoid` instance using homotopy classes of paths.

annotations.label: Groupoid reproduces fundamental group

anchor.snippet: It reproduces the fundamental group since

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroup X x := End (FundamentalGroupoid.mk x)` is literally the automorphism group at `x` in the fundamental groupoid.

annotations.label: van Kampen for fundamental groupoids

anchor.snippet: The van Kampen theorem admits a version for fundamental groupoids

annotations.note: Groupoid version of van Kampen is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Local systems correspond to representations of fundamental group

anchor.snippet: any local system

annotations.note: Local systems and the equivalence with representations of `π₁` are not formalized.

annotations.label: Étale fundamental group

anchor.snippet: the so-called étale fundamental group is used as a replacement for the fundamental group

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes an abstract Galois-category fundamental group (`IsFundamentalGroup F G`), but the étale fundamental group of a scheme is not constructed.

annotations.label: Étale fundamental group as profinite completion

anchor.snippet: the former is the profinite completion of the latter

annotations.note: Comparison between topological and étale fundamental groups (profinite completion) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of a root system / algebraic group

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group of a root system is defined in analogy to the computation for Lie groups

annotations.note: Fundamental group attached to a root system is not in Mathlib.
